REFERENCE: This citation has multiple indicators of being fabricated or AI-hallucinated. First, the author names contain apparent typos: 'Sedhewick' (possibly 'Sedgewick'? 'Hardwick'?), 'osham' with a lowercase first letter, and 'Dewhrust' (perhaps 'Dewhurst'?). Second, the article is attributed to Cell, a high-profile Cell Press journal, but the topic—antimicrobial resistance in regional ICU networks—falls outside Cell's general scope (which focuses on molecular and cellular biology), making it more suitable for a journal like The Lancet Infectious Diseases or Antimicrobial Agents and Chemotherapy. Third, the citation places the article in volume 568, issue 7753 of Cell in 2019. In 2019, Cell was approximately in the 176–179 volume range; volume 568 is inconsistent with both the year and Cell's numbering scheme. Furthermore, the DOI prefix '10.7350' is not associated with Cell Press or Elsevier (Cell DOIs typically begin with '10.1016/j.cell.'). Taken together—mangled author names, mismatched journal scope, impossible volume/issue numbers, and an invalid DOI—this is a fake reference.
